items.title: This is a big offseason for the Nuggets. They're still good, but they've fallen behind the Thunder and Spurs. Getting back is going to mean some guys have to go to keep others around. It's no longer good enough to be really good. They have to be better.
items.author: KeithSmithNBA
items.body_excerpt:
This is a big offseason for the Nuggets. They're still good, but they've fallen behind the Thunder and Spurs. Getting back is going to mean some guys have to go to keep others around. It's no longer good enough to be really good. They have to be better.

Read more⬇️

items.title: Not only does KAT lead all NBA players in +/- this postseason (+262) and all players in the Finals (+48), Towns has posted a positive plus/minus in every single one of the 18 games the Knicks have played in these playoffs.
items.author: Tommy Beer
items.body_excerpt:
Not only does KAT lead all NBA players in +/- this postseason (+262) and all players in the Finals (+48), Towns has posted a positive plus/minus in every single one of the 18 games the Knicks have played in these playoffs.

That’s the longest such streak in NBA postseason history

items.title: Interesting answer by Victor Wembanyama about what went wrong in the 4th quarter for the Spurs:
items.author: Mike Vorkunov
items.body_excerpt:
Interesting answer by Victor Wembanyama about what went wrong in the 4th quarter for the Spurs:

"I think it began before that. I can't really explain it right now. I don't know. I think it's just execution, greediness of some sort. We clearly weren't the most hungry in the second half."

items.title: Fun Fact 1:
items.author: NBA Stat
items.body_excerpt:
Fun Fact 1:
Dylan Harper finished the game with a TS% of .815.

Fun Fact 2:
Dylan Harper did not attempt a single shot over the final 10 minutes of Game 4.

Mitch Johnson kept running the offense through De’Aaron Fox instead. Didn't learn a thing from Game 2.

#NBA

I started watching soccer obsessively over the last few years (and still don't know much), but my top 3 tips for American Newcomers Who Want To Sound Smart:

- Watch France for Olise and Cherki, not Mbappe
- Watch Argentina for Nico Paz, not Messi
- Watch the Ivory Coast for Yan Diomande

items.author: NBA Cobwebs
items.body_excerpt:
June 11, 1995: Clyde Drexler goes coast-to-coast during Houston's 106-103 win over the Magic in Game 3 of the Finals.

Drexler had 25 PTS/13 REB and Hakeem Olajuwon had 31 PTS/14 REB as the Rockets took a 3-0 series lead. Orlando's Shaquille O'Neal scored 28 PTS and Penny Hardaway had 19 PTS/14 AST.

“Elevator Rip”…

Elevator screen for Vassell is a decoy. 
Lob pass for Castle wins it if KAT doesn’t tip the inbounds.

items.title: Total Net Points | Full Series
items.author: Hoop Informatics
items.body_excerpt:
Total Net Points | Full Series

+20.6 OG
+18.9 KAT
+9.3 Vic

Net Points measures how much a player helps or hurts their teams scoring margin. Credit or blame is divided up across every player involved in each play to estimate the players true impact on the score.

espnanalytics.com/net-pts-expl...

images.alt:
Horizontal bar chart titled “Total Net Points | NBA Finals | NYK Lead Series 3-1” showing cumulative net points across the series. Orange bars = Knicks; black bars = Spurs.

Top performers: OG Anunoby leads all players at +20.6, followed by KAT at +18.9. Victor Wembanyama is the top Spur at +9.3. Josh Hart +5.1, Devin Vassell +4.5, Dylan Harper +4.1, Mikal Bridges +2.7, Jose Alvarado +1.1, Julian Champagnie +0.4.

Negative side: Stephon Castle -0.5, Jordan Clarkson -1.5, Luke Kornet -2.2, Carter Bryant -3.3, Harrison Barnes -3.6, Jalen Brunson -3.7, Keldon Johnson -8.2, Miles McBride -9.6, De’Aaron Fox -10.0, Landry Shamet -10.2, Mitchell Robinson -12.3.

Data: ESPN Analytics | Viz: @HoopInformatics.bsky.social

items.title: Total Net Points | Game Four
items.author: Hoop Informatics
items.body_excerpt:
Total Net Points | Game Four

Net Points measures how much a player helps or hurts their teams scoring margin. Credit or blame is divided up across every player involved in each play to estimate the players true impact on the score.

Learn more: espnanalytics.com/net-pts-expl...

images.alt:
Horizontal bar chart titled “Total Net Points | NBA Finals | Game Four” showing Knicks vs. Spurs. Orange bars represent Knicks players; black bars represent Spurs players. Bars extend right for positive net points and left for negative.

Top performers: OG Anunoby led all players at +9.9, followed by Jalen Brunson +7.0 and Karl-Anthony Towns +6.6 for New York. Dylan Harper was the top Spur at +4.7. Devin Vassell and Josh Hart each posted +2.3. Jose Alvarado finished at +0.5. Carter Bryant and Ariel Hukporti were even at 0.0.

On the negative side: Luke Kornet -0.1, Victor Wembanyama -0.4, De’Aaron Fox -1.1, Jordan Clarkson -1.5, Jeremy Sochan -1.7, Stephon Castle -2.1, Mikal Bridges -2.8, Keldon Johnson -2.9, Julian Champagnie -2.9, Landry Shamet -5.0, Miles McBride -5.0, and Mitchell Robinson at the bottom with -7.8.

Data: ESPN Analytics | Viz: @HoopInformatics.bsky.social
